meaning: n.[音]赋格曲,朦胧状态,记忆丧失症v.谱赋格曲
examples: I'm going to play you a Bach fugue.
Sigh Fugue motherland, flowers of mind.
I've finished my Toccata but I have no fugue.
He presents a lament, and then a fugue, which attempts to resolve the suffering.
Then his hands froze and he lifted his head, gazed off to the side in a fugue of stillness, listening.
